purpose: Phase 5 continuous engine prototype — validates Normal-distribution replacement for d10 dice pool; re-runs Phase 4 Fast vs Strong test with continuous engine to determine whether continuous mapping alone resolves the Agi dominance problem. Trigger: Jordan accepted Path 3 (full continuous engine) on 2026-05-15.

entries.section: §dice — derived from face distribution at TN 7
entries.quoted_text: Face 1 = -1 success (0.1 prob), Face 2-6 = 0 successes (0.5 prob), Face 7-9 = +1 success (0.3 prob), Face 10 = +2 successes (0.1 prob). Expected value per die: 0.1*(-1) + 0.5*0 + 0.3*1 + 0.1*2 = 0.4.

entries.section: Architecture C — sim baseline matching v27 convention
entries.quoted_text: Architecture C — sim consistency with v27 sim that produced Agi-dominance baseline. Canonical formula is End*5 per params/combat.md L15.

entries.section: §Damage Formula — Light Blade Cut vs None tier

entries.quoted_text: STR multiplier (multiplicative): Light×1, Heavy×2, Blade×1, Blunt×1.5

entries.section: L91 (PP-717 D3) — discrete crit at net >=4; continuous mapping at magnitude >=4 (same threshold value, applied to continuous net)
entries.quoted_text: Critical Hit (net hits ≥ 4): weapon modifier doubled before applying armour reduction. (PP-717 — raised from ≥ 3.) In continuous engine, crit fires when continuous net >= 4.

entries.section: §degree resolution — Overwhelming requires net >= 2*Ob, magnitude mapping (defined for Phase 5 only; not yet canonical)
entries.quoted_text: [Phase 5 continuous engine mapping; not yet adopted as canon] Continuous magnitude threshold for Overwhelming: net - Ob >= 3.0

entries.section: §degree resolution — Partial requires net >= Ob/2, magnitude mapping (defined for Phase 5 only; not yet canonical)
entries.quoted_text: [Phase 5 continuous engine mapping; not yet adopted as canon] Continuous magnitude threshold for Partial: net - Ob >= -1.5

sim_limitations: Single weapon (Light Blade Cut) × single armour (None)
High draw rate at stamina cliff — conditional win rate is reliable signal
Degree magnitude bands are tentative (defined for sim purposes; not yet canonical)
key_finding: Continuous engine is statistically equivalent to discrete at canonical pool sizes (max deviation 0.029 in mean, 0.022 in std at pool 5-17). Agi-dominance persists in continuous engine (97.5% conditional Fast vs Strong, vs 97.8% discrete — within sampling noise). Continuous mapping alone does NOT resolve dominance — the doubling formula is the structural driver.
